sections.title: Question Tags — Introduction and Formula
sections.explanation: Question Tags is an essential building block of English grammar. Formula: Statement + comma + auxiliary verb (opposite polarity) + subject pronoun + ?. Rules: Positive statement takes a negative tag; negative statement takes a positive tag | Use the same auxiliary/verb tense as the main sentence | 'I am' uses tag 'aren't I?' as an exception | Question tags are used to confirm information or invite agreement
sections.formula: Statement + comma + auxiliary verb (opposite polarity) + subject pronoun + ?

examples.hindi: तुम आ रहे हो, है ना?
examples.english: You are coming, aren't you?

examples.hindi: वह नहीं आया, आया ना?
examples.english: He didn't come, did he?

examples.hindi: यह सुंदर है, है ना?
examples.english: This is beautiful, isn't it?

examples.hindi: तुम्हें यह पसंद नहीं है, है ना?
examples.english: You don't like this, do you?

examples.hindi: वह अच्छा गाता है, है ना?
examples.english: He sings well, doesn't he?

examples.hindi: हम मिलेंगे, ना?
examples.english: We will meet, won't we?

sections.title: Question Tags — Daily Life Usage
sections.explanation: Everyday sentences using Question Tags for daily conversation.
sections.formula: Statement + comma + auxiliary verb (opposite polarity) + subject pronoun + ?

examples.hindi: तुमने यह किया, किया ना?
examples.english: You did this, didn't you?

examples.hindi: वह डॉक्टर है, है ना?
examples.english: She is a doctor, isn't she?

examples.hindi: तुम यह कर सकते हो, कर सकते हो ना?
examples.english: You can do this, can't you?

examples.hindi: उसे यह पसंद है, है ना?
examples.english: She likes this, doesn't she?

examples.hindi: तुम भूखे नहीं हो, हो ना?
examples.english: You are not hungry, are you?

examples.hindi: यह मुश्किल नहीं है, है ना?
examples.english: This is not difficult, is it?

sections.title: Question Tags — Office & Professional Usage
sections.hindiTitle: Question Tags — ऑफिस और पेशेवर उपयोग
sections.explanation: Practice Question Tags in professional and office contexts.
sections.formula: Statement + comma + auxiliary verb (opposite polarity) + subject pronoun + ?

examples.hindi: तुमने खाना खा लिया, लिया ना?
examples.english: You have eaten food, haven't you?

examples.hindi: वे आएंगे, आएंगे ना?
examples.english: They will come, won't they?
I am right, aren't I?

examples.hindi: यह तुमने नहीं किया, किया ना?
examples.english: You didn't do this, did you?

sections.title: Question Tags — Negative and Question Forms
sections.hindiTitle: Question Tags — नकारात्मक और प्रश्न रूप
sections.explanation: How to make negative sentences and questions using Question Tags. Common mistakes are highlighted here.
sections.formula: Statement + comma + auxiliary verb (opposite polarity) + subject pronoun + ?

examples.hindi: वह वहाँ था, था ना?
examples.english: He was there, wasn't he?

examples.hindi: तुम स्कूल जाते हो, जाते हो ना?
examples.english: You go to school, don't you?

examples.hindi: वह जल्दी उठती है, उठती है ना?
examples.english: She wakes up early, doesn't she?

examples.hindi: हमें जल्दी जाना चाहिए, चाहिए ना?
examples.english: We should leave early, shouldn't we?
